message:
Add service, worker, and IPC infrastructure stubs

Implement the three-process architecture (§1.1) foundation and IPC protocol scaffolding.

## service/src/main.rs (§8.1: Windows service host)
Responsibilities (to be implemented):
- Register with Windows Service Control Manager using windows-service crate
- SERVICE_START handler: Initialize tokio runtime, volumes, scheduler, IPC server
- SERVICE_STOP handler: Graceful shutdown with pending job drain
- Run as LocalSystem or dedicated service account with SE_BACKUP_NAME privilege §11.1

Service configuration:
- Process priority: NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS §7.4
- Working directory: %PROGRAMDATA%\UltraSearch
- Dependencies: None (standalone service)

Startup sequence (to be coded):
1. Load config via core_types::config::load_config()
2. Initialize tracing (service::logging::init)
3. Discover NTFS volumes (ntfs-watcher::discover_volumes)
4. Open USN journals, load last_usn state from rkyv files §3.2
5. Spawn scheduler loop (tokio::spawn)
6. Start IPC named pipe server (ipc::start_server)
7. Report SERVICE_RUNNING to SCM

## service/src/logging.rs (§10.2: Tracing configuration)
Implements structured logging per LoggingSection config:

- `init(logging: &LoggingSection, data_dir: &str, process: &str)`:
  - Creates log directory if missing (e.g., C:\ProgramData\UltraSearch\log)
  - Sets up tracing_subscriber with EnvFilter (respects RUST_LOG override)
  - Configures file appender with daily rotation
  - Supports JSON (production) or text (development) formats
  - Includes target, file, line, thread IDs, and span events (CLOSE)
  - Keeps WorkerGuard in global OnceCell to prevent premature flush

Design notes:
- {data_dir} template substitution handles Windows path separators
- Non-blocking writer prevents log I/O from blocking hot paths
- RUST_LOG env var overrides config.logging.level for debugging
- ansi=false for JSON logs (machine-readable), ansi=true for text (console)

Usage in service/worker:
```rust
let cfg = load_config(None)?;
logging::init(&cfg.logging, &cfg.app.data_dir, "searchd")?;
tracing::info!("Service started");
```

## index-worker/src/main.rs (§8.2: Worker process contract)
Responsibilities (to be implemented):
- Parse CLI args: --job-file <path> --index-path <path>
- Load job file (rkyv/bincode batch of FileDescriptor)
- Initialize Extractous + optional IFilter/OCR components §6
- Open content-index Tantivy IndexWriter with configured heap/threads §5.3
- For each file in batch:
  - Extract text (content-extractor::extract)
  - Build Tantivy document with content field
  - Add to writer
- Commit index, write JobResult file, exit

Process configuration:
- Priority: IDLE_PRIORITY_CLASS or BELOW_NORMAL §7.4
- Memory limit: Optional job object cap (e.g., 1 GB max RSS)
- Timeout: Kill after N minutes if hung (service monitors)

Error handling:
- Per-file extraction errors: Log, skip, continue (don't fail entire batch)
- Commit errors: Write partial JobResult with error flag, exit non-zero
- Crash: Service detects exit code, backs off offending files

## ipc/src/lib.rs (§8.3: IPC protocol – named pipes + bincode)
Responsibilities (to be implemented):
- Server: tokio::net::windows::named_pipe::NamedPipeServer
  - Pipe name: \\.\pipe\ultrasearch-service
  - SDDL: O:SYG:SYD:(A;;0x12019f;;;AU)(A;;FA;;;SY) §11.2 (system full, authenticated users RW)
  - Accept loop: spawn per-connection task
- Client: Blocking or async NamedPipeClient for UI/CLI
- Framing: [u32 length][payload bytes] (bincode serialization)

Message types (to be defined):
```rust
enum Request {
    Search { id: u64, query_ast: QueryAst, limit: u32, mode: SearchMode },
    Status,
    ConfigGet { key: String },
    ConfigSet { key: String, value: String },
}

enum Response {
    SearchResults { id: u64, results: Vec<ResultRow>, truncated: bool },
    Status { volumes: Vec<VolumeStatus>, index_stats: IndexStats },
    Config { value: String },
    Error { message: String },
}
```

Concurrency:
- Multiple simultaneous pipe connections supported (Windows named pipes auto-instance)
- Each connection = dedicated tokio task reading requests, dispatching to handlers, writing responses
- Backpressure: Limit concurrent requests per connection (e.g., 10 in-flight max)

Protocol versioning:
- Handshake: Client sends { version: 1 }, server validates or rejects
- Future-proof: Add version field to all messages for forward compat

Security (§11.2):
- Pipe ACLs deny anonymous/everyone
- No authentication within protocol (relying on Windows identity via pipe)
- Input validation: Reject queries exceeding size limits, malformed ASTs

## Status
All files are minimal stubs with extensive doc comments outlining implementation contracts.
Establishes:
- Service ↔ Worker ↔ IPC boundaries
- Logging infrastructure ready for immediate use
- Named pipe protocol design for UI/CLI integration

Next steps:
- UI + CLI stubs (c00.2.5-c00.2.6)
- Service skeleton with volume discovery (c00.3.1)
- IPC message definitions and framing (c00.6.1)

Related: PLAN §8 (service/worker/IPC), §10.2 (logging), §11 (security), AGENTS.md (dotenvy)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+use anyhow::Result;
+use tracing_subscriber::{fmt, layer::SubscriberExt, util::SubscriberInitExt, EnvFilter};
+
+/// Initialize tracing/logging for the service process.
+///
+/// - Reads filter from `RUST_LOG` (default `info`).
+/// - Structured stdout for now; file appender will be added once paths are finalized.
+pub fn init_tracing() -> Result<()> {
+    let filter = EnvFilter::try_from_default_env().unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new("info"));
+
+    let fmt_layer = fmt::layer()
+        .with_target(true)
+        .with_thread_ids(true)
+        .with_file(false)
+        .with_line_number(false);
+
+    tracing_subscriber::registry()
+        .with(filter)
+        .with(fmt_layer)
+        .try_init()
+        .map_err(Into::into)
+}
